name: Walk from Molino Hundido to Reserva Natural de la Laguna de Salicor – circular from Campo de Criptana
name_original: Camindo del Molino Hundido a Reserva Natural de la Laguna de Salicor – circular desde Campo de Criptana
region_intro: Mountain biking around Campo de Criptana offers a unique experience across the distinctive La Mancha plains, characterized by vast, often arid landscapes. The region features iconic windmills perched on hilltops, providing varied terrain and panoramic views. Riders will encounter unpaved segments and natural wetlands, including the Laguna de Salicor, which add to the diverse natural features. This blend of cultural heritage and natural beauty creates a compelling backdrop for mountain bike trails. Best mountain bike trails around Campo De Criptana The most popular mountain bike trail is Wells and Water Route of Campo de Criptana, a 11.8 miles (18.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the local water features and historical wells. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Walk from Molino Hundido to Reserva Natural de la Laguna de Salicor – circular from Campo de Criptana, a moderate 33.4 miles (53.7 km) path. This route offers scenic views of the natural reserve and its unique wetland ecosystem. Local mountain bikers also love the Emisora TV Alcázar – Dirt Road Through Wildflowers loop from Campo de Criptana, a 46.1 miles (74.2 km) trail leading through dirt roads and wildflower fields, often completed in about 5 hours 27 minutes. Mountain biking around Campo de Criptana is defined by its iconic windmills, expansive La Mancha plains, and significant wetlands. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Campo de Criptana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Campo de Criptana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I find in Campo de Criptana?
A: Campo de Criptana offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, from easy to difficult. You'll find routes traversing the distinctive La Mancha plains, often featuring unpaved segments, and leading through areas with iconic windmills and natural wetlands. The terrain can vary, with some routes classified as 'hard' due to rugged sections that might require good fitness.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Campo de Criptana?
A: There are over 70 mountain bike trails around Campo de Criptana listed on komoot. These include 10 easy routes, 60 moderate routes, and 8 more challenging options, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Campo de Criptana?
A: Yes, Campo de Criptana offers several easy mountain bike trails suitable for families. One example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40325007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Emisora TV Alcázar – Windmills of Consuegra loop from Campo de Criptana&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 26.3 km route. The region also has a 10km trail connecting the town to Laguna de Salicor, which is suitable for cycling and walking.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Mountain bike trails in Campo de Criptana often pass by significant natural features and historical landmarks. You can expect to see the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2909432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills of Campo de Criptana&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6939582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de los Molinos Windmills&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views. Many routes also explore the region's wetlands, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4658585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Veguilla&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2992746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salicor Lagoons&lt;/a&gt;, which are vital habitats for migratory birds.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Campo de Criptana are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042106662/walk-from-molino-hundido-to-reserva-natural-de-la-laguna-de-salicor-circular-from-campo-de-criptana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walk from Molino Hundido to Reserva Natural de la Laguna de Salicor – circular from Campo de Criptana&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 53.7 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40317186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Emisora TV Alcázar – Dirt Road Through Wildflowers loop from Campo de Criptana&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 74.2 km route.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Campo de Criptana?
A: The mountain bike trails in Campo de Criptana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the unique landscapes, iconic windmills, and the blend of cultural heritage with natural beauty.

Q: What is a popular moderate mountain bike trail in Campo de Criptana?
A: A popular moderate trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041752113/wells-and-water-route-of-campo-de-criptana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wells and Water Route of Campo de Criptana&lt;/a&gt;. This 18.9 km route takes about 1 hour 36 minutes to complete and explores local water features and historical wells.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Ammunition Depot – Laguna Grande de Villafranca loop from Campo de Criptana&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 76.1 km route. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40317186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Emisora TV Alcázar – Dirt Road Through Wildflowers loop from Campo de Criptana&lt;/a&gt;, which is 74.2 km long.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Campo de Criptana?
A: The La Mancha region, where Campo de Criptana is located, experiences hot, dry summers. Therefore, the best seasons for mountain biking are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities. Winter can also be suitable, though some days might be colder.

Q: Are there any nature centers or reserves accessible by bike?
A: Yes, the Centro de la Naturaleza "Tierra de Gigantes" (Giants' Land Nature Center), located southwest of Campo de Criptana, is an 8.70-hectare center where visitors can explore native flora and fauna. It includes a bird nesting area and an artificial lake with an observatory, and is accessible by bicycle.

Q: Can I combine mountain biking with cultural sightseeing in Campo de Criptana?
A: Absolutely. Campo de Criptana is famously associated with Miguel de Cervantes' "Don Quixote" and its iconic windmills. Many mountain bike routes incorporate these historical landmarks, allowing you to enjoy both the natural landscape and the rich cultural heritage of the region. You can cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2909432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills of Campo de Criptana&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6939582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de los Molinos Windmills&lt;/a&gt;, which are designated Historic Sites.
tips: Currently, the Molino Infante, the Burleta, the Culebro, which houses the Sara Montiel Museum, the Chimera, which houses the Museum on Holy Week in the town and the Poyatos Mill, currently a tourist information point, can be visited.

The Hermitage or Sanctuary of the Holy Christ of Villajos is one of the most emblematic places in Campo de Criptana (Ciudad Real).
It originated from the old village of Villajos, which was abandoned in the 14th century. The original church was dedicated to Our Lady of Villajos.
This is a nice area with a narrow path where you can find shade and enjoy the flora and fauna.
